objectives: Name major body parts in Spanish
Communicate with a doctor or pharmacist

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Paciente
dialogue_blocks.text: Buenos días, doctor. Me siento muy mal.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Good morning, doctor. I feel very ill.

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Paciente
dialogue_blocks.text: Me duele mucho la cabeza y tengo fiebre desde ayer.
dialogue_blocks.translation: My head hurts a lot and I've had a fever since yesterday.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [me ˈðwele ˈmuʧo la kaˈβeθa i ˈteŋɡo ˈfjeβɾe ˈdesðe aˈʝeɾ]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Doctor
dialogue_blocks.text: ¿Tiene tos o le duele la garganta?
dialogue_blocks.translation: Do you have a cough or does your throat hurt?
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [ˈtjene ˈtos o le ˈðwele la ɣaɾˈɣanta]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Paciente
dialogue_blocks.text: Sí, tengo tos y también me duelen los huesos.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Yes, I have a cough and my bones ache too.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [si ‖ ˈteŋɡo ˈtos i tamˈbjen me ˈðwelen los ˈwesos]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Doctor
dialogue_blocks.text: Parece que tiene gripe. Le voy a recetar un medicamento.
dialogue_blocks.translation: It seems you have the flu. I'm going to prescribe you medication.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [paˈɾeθe ke ˈtjene ˈɡɾipe ‖ le ˈβoj a reθeˈtaɾ un meðikaˈmento]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Paciente
dialogue_blocks.translation: Do I need to rest at home?

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Doctor
dialogue_blocks.text: Sí, descanse dos días, tome mucha agua y evite el frío.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Yes, rest two days, drink lots of water and avoid the cold.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [si ‖ desˈkanse ðos ˈðias ‖ ˈtome ˈmuʧa ˈaɣwa i eˈβite el ˈfɾio]

exercises.instruction: Escoge la oración correcta para describir dolor (Choose correct pain expression)
exercises.question: How do you say 'My stomach hurts' in Spanish?

feedback.correct: ¡Exacto! 'Me duele el estómago' — the verb 'doler' works like 'gustar': it agrees with what hurts, not the person.
feedback.incorrect: 'Me duele el estómago' is the standard form. 'Doler' works like 'gustar' — you say 'me duele' (singular) or 'me duelen' (plural, e.g. los pies).

exercises.instruction: Une la parte del cuerpo con su descripción (Match body part to description)
Completa la conversación en la farmacia (Complete the pharmacy conversation)

dialogue.speaker: Cliente
dialogue.text: Buenas tardes, _____ mucho la garganta. (doler — me)

dialogue.speaker: Farmacéutico
dialogue.text: Le recomiendo este _____ para el dolor. (medicamento)

speaking_rubric.title: En el consultorio (At the Doctor's Office)
speaking_rubric.scenario: You don't feel well. Describe your symptoms to the doctor — what hurts, since when, and how you feel overall.
speaking_rubric.prompt: Describe tus síntomas al médico. ¿Qué te duele? ¿Desde cuándo?

scoring_criteria.description: Correct use of doler and tener for symptoms

sample_response.text: Doctor, me duele mucho la cabeza y tengo fiebre desde esta mañana. También me duele la garganta y me siento muy cansada.
sample_response.ipa: [me ˈðwele ˈmuʧo la kaˈβeθa i ˈteŋɡo ˈfjeβɾe ˈdesðe ˈesta maˈɲana]

cultural_notes.formality: In Mexico, it's common to say '¿Cómo te sientes?' (How do you feel?) as genuine concern. People don't rush health conversations — they take time to express care.
cultural_notes.gestures: Touching the relevant body part while describing pain is expected and helpful. Doctors in Mexico often ask '¿Le duele aquí?' while pressing gently.
cultural_notes.regional_variations: Mexicans commonly use 'me cayó mal la comida' (the food didn't agree with me) for stomach issues. For a headache, 'traigo dolor de cabeza' is also natural alongside 'me duele la cabeza'.
